C[Dragon Pie: With this move, the most natural follow up is to press my stone.  If I respond to solidify my stone he can build a position on the top side.  As it is, I've prevented him from having a shimari here, my stone has done it's job.  A move along the top is one that both of us want.  On top of that, if I get a position on the right, I may be able to pressure his group on the right.  I can't pressure it directly at the moment, but I welcome the possibility of doing so.  I will play high for improved influence and a greater chance at being able to pressure the group.  The bottom right also catches my eye as a place that I'd like to play, but alas, I only get to put one stone down at a time.

C[Dragon Pie: My 3-3 stone can be pressed, but it's also a solid position, so I like the prospects of attaching to the stones on top. If I have strong groups while my opponent's are weaker, I feel good.  I like my opponent to be the one straining while I relax and drink an icy margarita.
